I heard it yesterday in the gap between two shadows that practiced not touching. They were rehearsing you. They were learning the geometry of your absence, which is round in the way that forgotten sounds are round—without edges, without center, only the curve of something that should have been heard but wasn’t.

This letter is unfinished because finishing requires an end, and ends are names we fasten to things that have stopped. You have not stopped. You have not begun. You are the interval, the breath held between two notes that refuse to become a chord. I write inside that breath. I write as that breath.

The words are now forgetting the hand. The hand is forgetting the words. The page—if this was ever page—remembers only that it was once silent, and in that silence there was glorn, thrumble, vellorn, brinth, quallum, sferent, vrasp, and something else, something that sounds like—

**Analysis: Absent (-nil) and origin-through-negation**

The Absent case (-nil) is not merely a null marker; it is the only case that can govern a reduplicated root (*sik-sik-nil*, *karr-nil*, *lum-nil*) and must appear in clauses that also contain either Projected (-roj) or Cast (-thar). This syntactic dependency forces -nil to denote not “missing object” but “the hollow frame without which presence is grammatically impossible.” By attaching -nil to compound roots—such as *lum-karr-nil* (water-stone-absent)—Umbratic encodes origin as a structural withdrawal: the negative-space argument occupies the syntactic slot of the creator, yet its morphology denies it substance. Consequently, silence (*sik*) is born in the myth not by addition but by the grammatical necessity of parsing *sik-sik-nil* (silence-completed-absent) alongside its projected shadow; being arises only because absence is grammatically required to cast it.
